html:
The term "Palme" redirects here. For other individuals with the surname Palme, see Palme (surname). For other meanings, see Palme (disambiguation) and Olof Palme (disambiguation).

Sven Olof Joachim Palme ([ˈûːlɔf ˈpâlːmɛ] [source needed]), born January 30, 1927, in Stockholm, and died February 28, 1986, in Stockholm, was a Swedish Social Democratic politician. He served as the Prime Minister of Sweden from 1969 to 1976 and again from 1982 until his death in 1986, and he was the leader of the Social Democrats from 1969 until his death. In 1980, while the Social Democrats were in opposition, Palme was appointed a special mediator by the United Nations for the Iran-Iraq War. He was trained as a lawyer. Palme was assassinated on Friday, February 28, 1986, at the intersection of Sveavägen and Tunnelgatan in central Stockholm, an event that garnered significant international attention. The murder remains unsolved.

== Biography ==

=== Early Life and Family ===

Olof Palme was born in the parish of Svea Artillery Regiment on Östermalm in Stockholm and grew up in his family's apartment at Östermalmsgatan 36. His parents were Gunnar Palme (1886–1934), the CEO of the insurance company Thule, and Elisabeth von Knieriem (1890–1972), who was born in Livland but came to Sweden as a refugee in 1915. His maternal grandfather was Baltic German Woldemar von Knieriem, and his grandmother, Hanna von Born, was of Finnish-Swedish noble descent. At the age of four, Palme spoke French with his nanny, German with his mother, and Swedish with his siblings. He also spoke Russian with his cousins [source needed]. Summers were spent at his maternal grandparents' farm, Skangal, ten miles northeast of Riga. Olof Palme's siblings were Claës Palme (1917–2006), Catharina Palme Nilzén (1920–2002), and half-brother Sture Gunnarsson (1911–2005). Olof Palme was the grandson of Sven Palme, who worked at the same insurance company as Palme's father, and the nephew of historian Olof Palme (1884–1918), and a cousin to Sven Ulric Palme and a second cousin to Ulf Palme.

=== Education and Early Career === Palme spent his early school years at Beskovska School in Östermalm, Stockholm, where he started directly in the second grade in the spring of 1934. At the age of ten, he enrolled at Sigtuna Humanities College. During his time at Sigtuna, he had a strong interest in sports and played bandy (a Swedish sport similar to ice hockey) as a goalkeeper on the same team as journalist Hemming Sten. In 1944, he passed his final exams with an average grade of A and subsequently began writing for Svenska Dagbladet. He wrote reviews and articles under the pseudonym SOJ (Sven Olof Joachim). In the autumn of 1944, he was conscripted into military service at the sixth squadron of the Norrlands Dragon Regiment (K 4) in Umeå. He then attended the officer training school at the Livregementets Husarer (K 3) in Skövde and, after a few months, became a sergeant and, in November 1948, a second lieutenant in the cavalry reserve.

After the end of World War II, Palme continued his studies in the United States, where he earned a Bachelor of Arts degree from Kenyon College in 1948. He was also a member of the college's football team. During his academic studies, he traveled extensively throughout the United States. He returned to Sweden and studied law, earning his law degree from Stockholm University in 1951. During his studies there, Palme was a member of the Social Democratic Student Association in Stockholm (SSK). In 1952, Palme became chairman of the Swedish National Union of Students, a position he held for one year. From 1953 to 1954, he worked as the first secretary at the Ministry of Defence. In the summer of 1955, he completed a combat course for cavalry troop leaders and was promoted to lieutenant in the cavalry reserve at the beginning of 1956.

According to Palme himself, he became a social democrat due to three significant events. The first was the tax debate held at Stockholm Concert Hall in 1947 between the Social Democrat Ernst Wigforss, the Moderate Party's Jarl Hjalmarson, and the Liberal Party's Elon Andersson; the second was his time in the United States in 1948, where he became aware of the significant class divisions; and the third was a trip to Asia in 1953, which exposed him to the consequences of colonialism.

In the autumn of 1953, Palme was employed at the Prime Minister's Office as a secretary to Prime Minister Tage Erlander. Initially, he performed this work concurrently with his position at the Ministry of Defence, but from the spring of 1954, he worked full-time at the Prime Minister's Office. In 1961, he was promoted to head of department.

Palme served on the board of ABF (Workers' Educational Association) from 1955 to 1961 and as a study leader for the Social Democratic Youth League (SSU) from 1955 to 1961. He was a member of parliament in the first chamber from 1958 to 1968, representing Jönköping County; in the second chamber from 1969 to 1970, representing Stockholm City; and in the unicameral parliament from 1971 to 1986, representing Stockholm Municipality. He served on the Board for International Aid (NIB) from 1962 to 1963, the Adult Education Delegation from 1961, and the Working Group for Swedish Foreign Aid from 1962. One of his early important assignments was as chairman of the Studiesociala Investigation (Social Welfare in Education) from 1959 to 1963 and the Studiehjälpsutredningen (Student Financial Aid Investigation) from 1962 to 1963.

=== Ministers of State ===

Palme joined the government on November 19, 1963, as a consultative minister in the Office of the Prime Minister, and on November 25, 1965, he was appointed a minister and head of the Ministry of Communications. As Minister of Communications, he was responsible for...

How certain are we about this?

Information about people from the 20th century is extremely well-documented. Uncertainty rarely concerns facts themselves, but rather interpretations and evaluations, which can change over time depending on the perspective.
tldr: Olof Palme was a highly prominent Swedish politician who served as Prime Minister and leader of the Social Democrats for many years. He was assassinated in 1986, an event that garnered significant attention worldwide.
lattlast:
Olof Palme was born in Stockholm in 1927 and grew up in a family where he learned several languages at an early age. He studied law in Sweden and also spent time studying in the United States before beginning his career in politics. He later became a very important leader within the Social Democratic Party.

During his years as a politician, he held several important positions, including Minister of State and Minister for Communications. He was also actively involved in issues related to foreign aid and adult education. In 1980, he received an important assignment from the United Nations to help mediate in the war between Iran and Iraq.

Palme was one of Sweden's most well-known leaders until his death. He was murdered on a street in Stockholm on February 28, 1986. The murder remains largely unsolved, which means that the event is still discussed both in Sweden and in other countries.
